description_long: This object is a short sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade has a metallic, slightly bluish-gray finish, suggesting it is made of steel or a similar alloy. The hilt features a simple, flat crossguard and a cylindrical handle with a copper or bronze appearance, ending in a faceted pommel. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible engravings or decorative elements, indicating a focus on function over ornamentation. The proportions and style are reminiscent of a Roman gladius or similar short sword, suitable for close combat. The object appears robust and well-crafted, with clear distinctions between the blade, guard, handle, and pommel.
description: A straight, double-edged short sword with a bluish steel blade, copper-toned cylindrical handle, and a simple crossguard and pommel. The design is functional and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight, double-edged blade, copper handle, and flat crossguard.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the blade's thin profile and the hexagonal shape of the pommel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade, handle, and crossguard visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the blade's thin edge and the back of the handle and pommel.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, copper-toned, simple with faceted pommel
